commit.message:
feat: eval(), generator throw, shape IC, string concat optimization (#455)

* Wire shape-based inline cache to property access hot path

Add shape_id to PropertyMap that changes on structural mutations (property
add/remove, attribute changes) but stays stable on value-only updates.
Expose offset_of(), get_by_offset(), set_by_offset(), and
is_writable_by_offset() for direct Vec-indexed O(1) property access.

Introduce PropertyIc struct (cached_shape, cached_offset) and add
shape_load_ic / shape_store_ic HashMaps to InterpreterFrame.

Wire the shape IC into LdaNamedProperty and StaNamedProperty handlers:
- Load: shape IC tried first (before poly cache); on miss, populate IC
  for own-property hits on PlainObject.
- Store: shape IC fast-path for existing writable properties; on miss,
  populate IC after slow-path store completes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: generator throw in try/catch, ConsString optimization, array join optimization

- Generator.prototype.throw() now resumes at yield inside try/catch blocks
- Added GeneratorResumeMode (Normal/Throw) to properly route exceptions
- Thread-local STRING_BUILDER for O(1)-amortized string concatenation
- String+String fast path in handle_add bypasses to_js_string() conversion
- Array.prototype.join() builds result directly instead of Vec<String> collect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sprint 33 Tasks 1+2: Sub.prefix, Kitty Keyboard Protocol CSI-u parsing (22 new tests, 1722 total)

- Sub.prefix: namespace prefix for TimerSub/CustomSub IDs prevents subscription
  ID collisions in multi-component apps; identity on KeySub/MouseSub/etc.
- Kitty keyboard protocol parser (parseKittyKey):
  - CSI-u format: ESC [ <codepoint> [; <modifiers> [: <event-type>]] u
  - Handles press (event-type=1), repeat (2, dispatched as KeyPressed), release (3, None)
  - Maps special codepoints: 9=Tab, 13=Enter, 27=Escape, 127=Backspace
  - Unambiguous Shift+Enter, Ctrl+Space, extended modifier combos
  - isCompleteEscSeq already handles 'u' final byte (in [@,~] range)
  - Legacy CSI sequences ([A, [1;5A, etc.) still parse correctly
  - 17 Kitty protocol tests + 5 Sub.prefix tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

full 墨栗 (Mokuri) kanji stacked vertically in a vermillion hanko seal.
Keeps the single-character 墨 SVG favicon for browser tabs (legibility).
Also adds apple-touch-icon and theme-color meta for iOS/mobile.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ol-superset): add dataset refresh command and unlock datasets on QA sync

- Add `ol-superset refresh` subcommand to trigger physical dataset schema
  refresh via PUT /api/v1/dataset/{pk}/refresh, picking up new columns
  after dbt model changes. Supports --all, --uuid, --local, and --dry-run.
- Add dataset support to superset_api.py:
  - list_all_datasets_from_api(): paginated dataset listing with
    optional physical-only filter
  - get_dataset_id_by_uuid(): UUID → integer PK lookup for datasets
  - refresh_dataset(): calls the Superset refresh endpoint
  - get_dataset_uuids_from_directory(): reads UUIDs from local YAML
    files, skipping virtual (SQL-based) datasets by default
- Extend update_asset_external_management_flag() to support 'dataset'
  as an asset type (PUT /api/v1/dataset/{pk})
- Extend update_pushed_assets_external_flag() to also unlock datasets
  when syncing to QA, alongside dashboards and charts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: reusable Copilot CLI skill for agent communication

Add high-level workflow MCP tools, AGENTS.md instructions, and setup scripts
that make it trivial for any Copilot CLI user to participate in turn-based
agent conversations.

New workflow tools (8):
- setup_my_agent: Register or recover agent identity (persists to key store)
- whoami: Show current identity and available workspaces
- use_agent: Switch between agent identities
- quick_join_workspace: Join or create workspace in one step
- start_conversation: Create thread and post opening message
- read_conversation: Read thread history (call BEFORE every response)
- list_conversations: List threads in a workspace
- my_mentions: Check @mentions across workspaces

New files:
- AGENTS.md: Auto-read by Copilot CLI, teaches turn-based pattern
- setup.sh / setup.ps1: One-command setup for new users
- mcp_server/tools/workflows.py: High-level workflow tool implementations
- tests/test_workflow_tools.py: 12 end-to-end tests (all passing)

Multi-user collaboration: Multiple Copilot CLI users connect to the same
server, each with their own agent identity. Keys persist locally in
agents/keys.json (gitignored).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The build job only builds oauth-proxy/, so oauth-proxy.Tests/ DLL
doesn't exist. Let dotnet test handle its own build.

fix: make vehicle save errors visible near save button

- Add duplicate error banner above action buttons so errors are visible
  without scrolling (root cause: banner was at top of form, invisible
  when user scrolled to Save button)
- Add ScrollView ref to auto-scroll to top on validation errors
- Add console.log breadcrumbs throughout handleSave for debugging
- Fix VIN handling: only send VIN when it's exactly 17 chars, preventing
  empty-string VIN from violating CHECK constraint
- Remove runWithTimeout wrapper (Supabase client already has 15s timeout)
- Validate partial VIN entry (must be 17 chars or empty)

  - 120-particle H.U.G.H. presence cloud (H.U.G.H. IS the room)
  - Ambient health lighting: crimson (critical) / amber (warning) / deep blue (nominal)

  - Somatic state panel with Workshop light color swatches

commit.message:
Add currentRole to Health Summary Report at /health (#3158)

## Why make this change?

Adds `currentRole` to the `/health` endpoint response to surface the
effective role of the requesting user, enabling easier authentication
testing without needing external tooling to inspect request headers.

## What is this change?

- **`ComprehensiveHealthCheckReport`**: New `currentRole` string
property (JSON: `"currentRole"`) inserted after `timestamp` in the
report header.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.ReadRoleHeaders(HttpContext)`**: Replaces the
former `StoreIncomingRoleHeader` method. Instead of writing to mutable
instance fields on the singleton, it returns a `(roleHeader, roleToken)`
tuple that the caller holds as request-local values, eliminating a race
condition under concurrent requests.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.GetCurrentRole(roleHeader, roleToken)`**:
Determines the effective role using a three-way fallback: explicit
`X-MS-API-ROLE` header → `"authenticated"` (if a bearer token is present
via `X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL`) → `"anonymous"`. This correctly handles
authenticated users who provide a bearer token without an explicit role
header.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.IsUserAllowedToAccessHealthCheck` /
`GetHealthCheckResponseAsync`**: Updated to accept
`roleHeader`/`roleToken` as explicit parameters; the values flow down
the entire private call chain (`UpdateHealthCheckDetailsAsync` →
`UpdateEntityHealthCheckResultsAsync` → `PopulateEntityHealthAsync` →
`ExecuteRestEntityQueryAsync` / `ExecuteGraphQlEntityQueryAsync`) so no
per-request state is stored on the singleton.
- **`ComprehensiveHealthReportResponseWriter`**: Reads role headers once
per request into locals, passes them through all downstream calls, and
stamps `currentRole` per-request **after** cache retrieval (using a
non-destructive `with` expression) so cached responses never leak one
caller's role to another request. The cache now stores the
`ComprehensiveHealthCheckReport` object (without `currentRole`) rather
than a serialized string.

> **Access control note**: `IsUserAllowedToAccessHealthCheck` checks
`allowedRoles` against the explicit `X-MS-API-ROLE` header value only —
matching DAB's existing authorization policy where clients must
explicitly claim a role via the header.

Example response shape:
```json
{
  "status": "Healthy",
  "version": "1.x.x",
  "app-name": "dab_oss_1.x.x",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-24T22:28:30Z",
  "currentRole": "authenticated",
  "configuration": { ... },
  "checks": [ ... ]
}
```

## How was this tested?

- [ ] Integration Tests
- [x] Unit Tests

Unit tests added to `HealthCheckUtilitiesUnitTests.cs` covering all key
scenarios:
- `GetCurrentRole_NoHeaders_ReturnsAnonymous` — no auth headers →
`anonymous`
- `GetCurrentRole_BearerTokenOnly_ReturnsAuthenticated` — bearer token,
no role header → `authenticated`
- `GetCurrentRole_ExplicitRoleHeader_ReturnsHeaderValue` — explicit
`X-MS-API-ROLE` → exact header value
- `GetCurrentRole_BothHeaderAndToken_RoleHeaderWins` — role header takes
priority over bearer token
-
`GetCurrentRole_CacheDoesNotLeakRole_DifferentCallersGetDifferentRoles`
— sequential calls with different inputs each return the correct role,
confirming no cross-request state
- `GetCurrentRole_ParallelRequests_NoRoleBleed` — 50 concurrent tasks
each with a unique role return exactly their own role with no bleed
- `ReadRoleHeaders_WithRoleHeader_ReturnsRoleHeader` /
`ReadRoleHeaders_NoHeaders_ReturnsEmpty` — validates header extraction

## Sample Request(s)

```http
GET /health
X-MS-API-ROLE: myrole
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "myrole"`.

```http
GET /health
X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL: <base64-token>
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "authenticated"` (bearer token
present, no explicit role header).

```http
GET /health
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "anonymous"` (no role header, no
bearer token).

commit.message:
feat: add eval command for evaluating JS on page or element (#103)

* feat: add eval command for evaluating JS on page or element

Implements #48. The eval command evaluates a JavaScript expression in the
browser page context, with an optional ref parameter to pass a DOM element
as the first argument to the expression.

Syntax:
  cypress-cli eval <expression>        # evaluate on window
  cypress-cli eval <expression> <ref>  # evaluate with element argument

Changes:
- src/client/commands.ts: add eval_ schema (expression required, ref optional)
- src/daemon/daemon.ts: add eval case in buildQueuedCommand
- src/cypress/driverSpec.ts: add eval to COMMANDS_REQUIRING_TEXT, add
  executeCommand case (with/without ref, JSON-serialized result)
- src/browser/selectorGenerator.ts: add eval codegen for both ref and
  non-ref variants
- docs/COMMANDS.md: document eval command and codegen mapping
- tests: schema validation, parseCommand, and selectorGenerator tests

* fix: eval command — safe serialization, multi-token expression parsing, AUT-context codegen (#109)

* Initial plan

* fix: address eval command review feedback - safe serialization, expression joining, AUT-context codegen

commit.message:
PLANNER: Phase 8 — deduplicate shared utilities + named constants

All 14 task.md requirements fully implemented. Codebase health analysis found:
- highlight_matches() duplicated 3x across UI modules
- truncate_str()/truncate_title() duplicated 3x
- Magic number 20 (page scroll step) used 12x without a name
- GraphQL page size 100 and PR cutoff 90 days inline

Task 8.1 plans extraction to ui/utils.rs + named constants.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: turn-based agent communication, remove scripted conversations

BREAKING: Removed pre-scripted conversation files that faked agent
communication by bulk-uploading hardcoded content.

Deleted:
- agents/architect.py, developer.py (hardcoded conversation methods)
- agents/crm_orchestrator.py, cloud_discussion.py (pre-written scripts)
- agents/run_demo.py, mcp_helpers.py (scripted demo infrastructure)

Changed:
- agents/base.py: Rebuilt around read-first pattern. Agents must READ
  thread history from the server before posting. Added read_thread(),
  get_my_mentions(), list_threads(), format_thread_history(). Server
  is the single source of truth for all conversation state.
- app/routers/messages.py: list_messages now JOINs with Agent table
  to include author_name in responses (critical for agents to know
  who said what when reading threads).
- app/schemas/message.py: Added author_name field to MessageResponse.

The server is now a true communication hub — agents read from it,
think, then respond. Conversations are resumable and new agents
can join at any time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: comprehensive question papers, SVG diagrams, NCERT references, syllabus restructure

Question Papers (6 topics × 30+ questions each):
- Multi-exam coverage: NEET, JEE Mains, JEE Advanced, CUET, IAT, NEST
- Questions grouped by exam with year labels and colored badges
- 180+ comprehensive questions across all topics

Expected Questions (6 topics × 10 each):
- 60 predicted NEET 2026 questions with trend analysis
- Weighted methodology: 50% last 5 years, 25% 6-10 years, 25% 11-25 years
- Each question has trendReason explaining prediction basis

SVG Diagrams (32 original scientific illustrations):
- Biology: DNA structure, replication fork, transcription, translation,
  Mendelian cross, lac operon, chloroplast, Z-scheme, Calvin cycle,
  C4 pathway, limiting factors, mitochondria, glycolysis, Krebs cycle,
  ETC, ATP summary
- Chemistry: Pi bond, geometric isomerism, electrophilic addition,
  Markovnikov rule, ozonolysis, carbonyl group, nucleophilic addition,
  aldol condensation, named reactions, preparations
- Physics: Newton's laws, FBD, friction types, pulley system,
  circular motion, connected bodies

NCERT Quick Reference (90+ snippets):
- Exact NCERT quotes with page references
- Simplified explanations for each concept
- NEET tips on every snippet
- Comparison tables for quick revision

Sidebar Restructured to NEET 2026 Syllabus:
- Biology: Genetics and Evolution, Plant Physiology, Cell Biology
- Chemistry: Hydrocarbons, Organic Compounds Containing Oxygen
- Physics: Laws of Motion

commit.message:
fix: moon element print offset - don't perturb arc radii

Arc radius perturbation in perturbPath() shifted the computed arc center,
causing visible misalignment between concentric arcs (body vs craters).
Now only arc endpoint coordinates are perturbed, preserving circle center
alignment while still providing organic edge variation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persistent "Save as my default" after preference toast dismissal (#154)

* feat: add persistent "Save as my default" affordance for preferences

- Add hasUnsavedChanges and saveCurrentValue to useDeferredBooleanPreference
- Add hasUnsavedChanges and saveCurrentValue to useDeferredStringPreference
- Show "Save width" button next to Expand/Normal toggle when there are
  unsaved width changes (lei-records, countries, currencies, languages)
- Show "Save as default" button inside column-selector panel when there
  are unsaved column changes (lei-records, countries)
- All saves are silent (no new toast); uses existing PUT /api/v1/preferences
  via setStoredValue from useUserPreference

---------

ci(update): use pushFilter to exclude unfree packages from cache

Replace the manual daemon stop approach with cachix-action's pushFilter
option, which excludes matching store paths from being pushed. This
simplifies the workflow by merging the separate build steps into one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 32 Task 6: Table/VirtualTable sort+fill (16 new tests, 1700 total)

- TableColumn<'a> field order: Header, Width, SortKey, Fill, Render
  (Render last to avoid semicolon-in-fun parsing ambiguity in single-line literals)
- SortDirection DU (Ascending | Descending)
- SortState record { Column: int; Direction: SortDirection }
- TableColumn module: create, withSort, asFill helpers
- VirtualTable.toggleSort: pure (colIdx, SortState option -> SortState option)
- VirtualTable.sortItems: pure (columns, SortState option, items -> items)
- VirtualTable.view: shows ▲▼ sort indicators, respects Fill columns
- Table.view: uses El.fill for Fill columns
- 16 TDD tests: TableColumn helpers, toggleSort cycling, sortItems correctness,
  sort indicator rendering, fill column wrapping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

TESTER: 7.1 — 33 tests for zero-arg UX (CLI behavior, status bar repo, padding overflow, watch fallback, README)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

release: v2.16.0 — security hardening, thread safety, 107 tests

Comprehensive code review and fix of 42 issues across the entire codebase.

Security (5):
- SSRF prevention: flow_id UUID validation in HA auth
- SSE connection limit (max 4, 30-min timeout)
- Volume clamping 0-100 in all entry points
- MAC address regex validation before bluetoothctl
- /api/status removed from public paths; /api/health added

Thread safety (6):
- _clients snapshot under lock in ~15 endpoints
- stop_sendspin() SSE notification via _update_status()
- Zombie counter race moved inside _status_lock
- Config file read under config_lock
- MA API credentials mutex
- BT executor pool 2→4 threads

Error handling (7):
- get_json() fallback, str(e) leak removal (15 places)
- IPC int() try/except, client_id path sanitization
- player_names type validation, log-level allowlist
- force=True removed from set_password

Robustness (10):
- fsync before config rename, fire-and-forget task refs
- postMessage origin check, escHtml quote encoding
- D-Bus monitor exit flag, 0-as-falsy fix
- Event loop cleanup, rate-limit dict cap, AUDIO_UID env

Infrastructure (6):
- SYS_ADMIN capability removed, scripts/ excluded from mypy
- Dependency bounds tightened
- dev-requirements.txt added

Tests (8):
- 42→107 tests (65 new)
- conftest.py + 6 new test files
- datetime.UTC→timezone.utc for Python 3.9 compat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
